Successfully downloaded image from URL; verified as real JPEG (800x533, 116KB) from Unsplash
install_gimp.sh installs GIMP package (2.10 version); setup_gimp.sh creates .config/GIMP/2.10 directories
setup_sharpen_task.sh line 37 mentions 'Filters → Enhance → Sharpen (Unsharp Mask)'; GIMP documentation confirms this is the standard sharpen filter
verifier.py calculate_sharpness_score() function (lines 27-60) implements Laplacian variance; this is a well-established method in image processing for sharpness assessment per research literature
verifier.py line 274: sharpness_improved = sharpness_improvement >= 10.0; this is mathematically sound and testable
verifier.py line 280: edge_strength_improved = edge_improvement >= 5.0; uses gradient magnitude calculation (lines 63-96)
verifier.py line 134: over_sharpened = noise_increase_ratio > 3.0; line 132 explains >300% noise increase indicates over-sharpening
verifier.py line 168: 'meaningfully_changed': change_percentage > 2; threshold is >2% not ≥2%

pi_items.source: verifier.py line 298: passed = score >= 75; score calculated as (criteria_met / total_criteria) * 100 where total_criteria = 4

pi_items.verified_value: Real images from Unsplash free stock photo service
pi_items.source: Both Unsplash URLs are accessible and return real JPEG images; Unsplash is a public, free stock photo service with Creative Commons Zero license

privileged_info_summary: This is a real GIMP image sharpening task using authentic Unsplash stock images (photo IDs 1506905925346-21bda4d32df4 and 1441974231531-c6227db76b6e) as the input data. The environment runs GIMP 2.10 on a 1920x1080 GNOME desktop. Success is determined by verifying sharpness improvement ≥10% and edge strength improvement ≥5% using Laplacian variance and gradient magnitude metrics (legitimate image processing techniques). The task requires meeting 3 of 4 criteria (including preventing over-sharpening with noise ratio <3.0 and ensuring ≥2% pixel changes) with a 90-second timeout and maximum 10 steps. Data is non-synthetic (from public Unsplash source). Minor note: meaningful change threshold uses strict inequality (>2%) not ≥2%.
